title: Purification of Viral RNA Using MagMAX Viral RNA Isolation Kit
author: Erika Bujaki

steps.title: DTT solution (1M) Dissolve 0
steps.instruction: DTT solution (1M) Dissolve 0.6168g of DTT in 4 ml of Nuclease Free water. Sterilize by filtration with 0.45 µm syringe filter unit and a 5 ml syringe. Prepare aliquots and store at -20°C.

steps.title: To process samples from FTA cards,...
steps.instruction: To process samples from FTA cards, prepare all the reagents listed, to start from liquid cell culture isolates prepare reagents in steps 1.3-1.6 only.

steps.title: MVRI_Flex_SV_300ul
steps.instruction: MVRI_Flex_SV_300ul.bdz Read the King Fisher Flex instrument manual for installation and operating instructions in its entirety before operating the magnetic particle processor. Import the attached MVRI_Flex_SV_300ul program and run a mock test. Up to 96 samples can be extracted at the same time in one plate using the 96-tip magnetic head. Plasticware for small volume sample extractions on King Fisher Flex equipped with 96-tip magnetic head. %s %s

steps.title: RNA Processing Buffer Combine the components...
steps.instruction: RNA Processing Buffer Combine the components below to prepare enough RNA Processing Buffer for the required number of samples and store on ice. RNA Processing Buffer can also be made ahead of time and stored at -20 °Cfor up to six months. %s %s

steps.title: Follow the below steps if using FTA cards
steps.instruction: Follow the below steps if using FTA cards. For liquid isolates, progress to RNA extraction section following workflow A/B/C depending on the mode of extraction.

steps.title: Wash Solution 1 Add indicated volume...
steps.instruction: Wash Solution 1 Add indicated volume of 100% Isopropanol to the bottle of Wash Solution 1 Concentrate. Mix well by inverting at least 5 times and mark bottle to indicate that the alcohol was added.

steps.title: Wash Solution 2 Add indicated volume...
steps.instruction: Wash Solution 2 Add indicated volume of 100% ethanol to the bottle of Wash Solution 2 Concentrate. Mix well by inverting at least 5 times and mark the bottle to indicate that ethanol was added. Taking an aliquot of the prepared Wash Solutions is recommended to avoid potential contamination if they are to be used on multiple occasions.

steps.title: Lysis/Binding Solution Defrost Carrier RNA on...
steps.instruction: Lysis/Binding Solution Defrost Carrier RNA on ice and combine the components listed below in the order indicated. Prepare enough reagents for the number of samples extracted that day, including controls, with adding extra 10% for pipetting loss. Add Carrier RNA to Lysis/Binding Solution Concentrate according to the table below, and mix briefly %s %s Add 100% isopropanol and mix well by vortexing. %s %s Prepare the Lysis/Binding Solution on the day it will be used. Keep at room temperature with a tightened lid

steps.title: Bead Mix Vortex the nucleic acid...
steps.instruction: Bead Mix Vortex the nucleic acid binding beads well to ensure that the beads are fully resuspended. Combine the components that are listed below: %s %s Mix well by vortexing and place the prepared bead mix on top of ice or in the fridge until it is needed, but avoid freezing as it destroys its properties. Prepare the bead mix on the day it will be used. Bead mix can be stored on ice until it is needed for up to 4 hours. Avoid freezing the mixture as it damages the magnetic beads.

steps.title: The indicated processing times are based...
steps.instruction: The indicated processing times are based on handling 12 samples at the same time .

steps.title: Prepare the lysate
steps.instruction: Prepare the lysate: For each sample: Set up and label 1.5 ml Eppendorf DNA LoBind centrifuge tubes, then aliquot 400 μl of the lysis/binding solution (supplemented with carrier RNA and 100% Isopropanol - see Reagent preparation section) into each tube. 2. Transfer 200 μl of the liquid isolate or the full recoverable volume from the processed FTA card supernatant to the labelled tubes containing the Lysis/Binding Solution (supplemented with carrier RNA and 100% Isopropanol). When adding sample, check and confirm the sample ID. Immerse pipette tips slightly in the Lysis/Binding Solution to prevent creating aerosols and rinse pipette tip. Keep all other tubes closed to avoid cross contamination. 3. Mix gently by vortexing for 30s and spin briefly to collect tube content.

steps.title: Add 14 μl of prepared bead...
steps.instruction: Add 14 μl of prepared bead mix to each sample tube containing the lysed sample solution. Mix bead solution by pipetting to avoid settling down of the magnetic beads. Use a new tip for each addition to minimise bead loss and rinse the tip gently a few times to ensure full volume is transferred.

steps.title: Mix tubes thoroughly at gentle speed...
steps.instruction: Mix tubes thoroughly at gentle speed for 4 min to fully lyse viruses and bind RNA to beads. A shaker or rotator mixer can be used for this step or tubes can be repeatedly rotated or gently vortexed manually. It is important to achieve sufficient mixing in this step with a visibly homogenous coloured mixture throughout to ensure efficient lysis and bead binding.

steps.title: Centrifuge tubes briefly to collect content,...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge tubes briefly to collect content, then place on magnet and leave for at least 2 minutes to allow for bead capture to complete. Beads should form a pellet against the magnet. Pellets may also be smeared on the tube’s wall as well as forming a compact body. Twisting the tube gently help to make the pellet more compact if needed.

steps.title: Carefully aspirate and discard supernatant without disturbing the beads
steps.instruction: Carefully aspirate and discard supernatant without disturbing the beads.

steps.title: It is important to remove the...
steps.instruction: It is important to remove the lysis supernatant fully, so a brief centrifugation before collecting the remaining supernatant might be necessary.

steps.title: Remove tubes from magnetic stand and...
steps.instruction: Remove tubes from magnetic stand and place in tube rack for washing with Wash Solution

steps.title: Add 200 µl Wash Solution 1...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µl Wash Solution 1 to each sample and vortex at moderate speed for 30s. Pellets do not necessarily get fully resuspended in all samples.

steps.title: Centrifuge briefly to collect tube content
steps.instruction: Centrifuge briefly to collect tube content.

steps.title: Capture beads on magnet until mixture...
steps.instruction: Capture beads on magnet until mixture becomes clear, indicating full capture.

steps.title: Carefully aspirate and discard supernatant
steps.instruction: Carefully aspirate and discard supernatant.

steps.title: Repeat steps 9-13 one more time...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 9-13 one more time to complete two washes with Wash Solution

steps.title: Remove tube from magnetic stand and...
steps.instruction: Remove tube from magnetic stand and place in tube rack for washing with Wash Solution

steps.title: Add 300 µl Wash Solution 2...
steps.instruction: Add 300 µl Wash Solution 2 to each sample and vortex at moderate speed for 30s. Beads can appear granular during washing steps with Wash Solution

steps.title: Centrifuge briefly to collect tube content
steps.instruction: Centrifuge briefly to collect tube content.

steps.title: Capture beads on magnet for 2...
steps.instruction: Capture beads on magnet for 2 mins or until mixture becomes clear, indicating full capture.

steps.title: Carefully aspirate and discard supernatant
steps.instruction: Carefully aspirate and discard supernatant.

steps.title: Repeat steps 15-19 to complete two washes with Wash Solution
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 15-19 to complete two washes with Wash Solution

steps.title: It is important to completely remove...
steps.instruction: It is important to completely remove the supernatant after the second wash to avoid inhibition in downstream applications.

steps.title: MVRI_DUO_SV_300ul
steps.instruction: MVRI_DUO_SV_300ul.bdz Read the King Fisher Duo Prime instrument manual for installation and operating instructions in its entirety before operating the magnetic particle processor. Import the attached MVRI_Duo_SV_300ul program and run a mock test. Up to 12 samples can be extracted at the same time in one plate using the 12-tip magnetic head. Plasticware for small volume sample extractions on King Fisher Duo Prime equipped with 12-tip magnetic head. %s %s

steps.title: Centrifuge briefly and remove any residual...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge briefly and remove any residual solution with a small volume fine-tipped pipette, without disturbing the pellet.

steps.title: Dry the beads by leaving the...
steps.instruction: Dry the beads by leaving the tube open for 2 minutes to allow any remaining alcohol to evaporate. Avoid overdrying the beads to the point of the pellet cracking and crumbling as this may lower the efficiency of nucleic acid recovery.

steps.title: Add 50 µL Elution Buffer to...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL Elution Buffer to each sample and shake/vortex vigorously for 4 min to fully resuspend the pellet.

steps.title: Centrifuge briefly to collect tube content
steps.instruction: Centrifuge briefly to collect tube content.

steps.title: Capture the beads on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Capture the beads on the magnet as before and collect supernatant containing the purified RNA in labelled containers and keep on ice for immediate use or store frozen until needed. Open tubes before placing on the magnet to avoid tube content flipping on sidewall. When this happens, remove tube and centrifuge briefly before replacing on the magnet. If beads are accidentally collected, return fluid from the pipette tip, and try again once supernatant is fully clear. Pellet might be smeared around the bottom of the tube instead of forming a compact pellet against the magnet. Look into the tube from above to check if eluate has fully cleared. Aim to collect RNA from a central position without touching the pellet.

steps.title: Aliquot 400 μl of the Lysis/Binding...
steps.instruction: Aliquot 400 μl of the Lysis/Binding Solution supplemented with carrier RNA and 100% Isopropanol (see step 1 and 2 in Reagent preparation) to the top row (row A) of the 96 deep-well plate.

steps.title: Add 200 μl of prepared wash...
steps.instruction: Add 200 μl of prepared wash solution 1 to rows B and C of the 96 deep well plate.

steps.title: Add 300 μl of prepared wash...
steps.instruction: Add 300 μl of prepared wash solution 2 to rows D and E of the 96 deep well plate.

steps.title: Place a 12-tip comb in a...
steps.instruction: Place a 12-tip comb in a 96 deep well plate in Row H.

steps.title: Add 60 μl of Elution buffer/...
steps.instruction: Add 60 μl of Elution buffer/ nuclease free water to the elution strip tube.

steps.title: Move the plate to the sample...
steps.instruction: Move the plate to the sample handling area and transfer 200 μl of the liquid isolate or all the recovered volume (200-240 μl) of the processed FTA card supernatant to the same top row (row A) of the 96 deep-well plate containing the Lysis/Binding Solution. Mix by gently pipetting up and down in the 96 deep-well plate a few times. (Discard tube containing the punches). When adding sample, check and confirm the sample ID. Immerse pipette tips slightly in the lysis/binding solution to prevent creating aerosols and rinse pipette tip. Keep all other tubes closed to avoid cross contamination.

steps.title: Add 14 μl of prepared bead...
steps.instruction: Add 14 μl of prepared bead mix to row A of the 96 deep well plate containing the lysed sample solution using a new tip for every addition and rinsing it with the sample solution. Mix bead solution by pipetting to avoid settling down of the magnetic beads. Use a new tip for each addition and rinse the tip gently a few times to ensure full volume is transferred.

steps.title: Check to confirm that the KingFisher...
steps.instruction: Check to confirm that the KingFisher Duo Prime is set up with 12-tip magnet and heating block. Follow the below steps to change the magnetichead on the King Fisher Duo Prime if necessary : Select and start Change Magnetic Head protocol in Maintenance protocols in the device menu. (This will position the magnet to be accessible.) Unscrew and remove the screws holding the magnetic head in place and lift the magnetic head to take it out. Replace the required magnetic head and tighten the screws to hold it in place. Remember to also change the heating blocks as the machine doesn’t give a prompt to do so! Run Check 12 tip protocol with a dummy test plate containing the tip comb in the required row to ensure the right positioning. Unload the test plate.

steps.title: Load the prepared sample plate onto...
steps.instruction: Load the prepared sample plate onto the King Fisher Duo Prime as prompted, ensuring the right orientation by matching the A1 marking on the turntable. Ensure that the plate is lying completely flat.

steps.title: Place the prepared elution strip into...
steps.instruction: Place the prepared elution strip into the device in the metal rack next to the loaded plate and use the fold over lock on the elution block to secure it in place. Use the position of the small round hole on the elution strip to match the red conical protrusion on the rack to ensure the right orientation. Both the plate and the elution strip should be on the same side of the turntable.

steps.title: Select the MVRI_DUO_SV_300ul Protocol and press start
steps.instruction: Select the MVRI_DUO_SV_300ul Protocol and press start.

steps.title: Close the front lid while the KingFisher is running
steps.instruction: Close the front lid while the KingFisher is running.

steps.title: After completion of the run, a final prompt will appear
steps.instruction: After completion of the run, a final prompt will appear. “Unload RNA plate and RNA Elution Strip”.

steps.title: Unload the elution strip containing the...
steps.instruction: Unload the elution strip containing the RNA, cap and place the elution strip containing the RNA on ice.

steps.title: Remove the sample plate from the...
steps.instruction: Remove the sample plate from the device then press the “Check Mark”. Discard the plate and content following the appropriate laboratory procedure. Wipe clean equipment, then switch on UV light for disinfection.

steps.title: Transfer eluted viral RNA to labelled...
steps.instruction: Transfer eluted viral RNA to labelled containers and keep on ice for immediate use or store frozen until needed.

steps.title: Aliquot 400 μl of the Lysis/Binding...
steps.instruction: Aliquot 400 μl of the Lysis/Binding solution supplemented with carrier RNA and 100% Isopropanol (see step 9 and 10 in Reagent preparation) to a 96 deep-well plate marked as Sample plate.

steps.title: Add the recovered volume (200-240 μl)...
steps.instruction: Add the recovered volume (200-240 μl) of samples to the 96 deep-well plate containing the Lysis/Binding Solution. Mix by gently pipetting up and down a few times. (Discard tube containing the punches). When adding sample, check and confirm the sample ID. Immerse pipette tips slightly in the lysis/binding solution to prevent creating aerosols and rinse pipette tip. Keep all other tubes closed to avoid cross contamination.

steps.title: Add 14 μl of prepared bead...
steps.instruction: Add 14 μl of prepared bead mix to the wells of the Sample plate containing the Lysed sample solution using a new tip for every addition and rinsing it with the sample solution to prevent loss of beads.

steps.title: Check to confirm that the instrument...
steps.instruction: Check to confirm that the instrument is set up with 96 deep-well magnetic head and 96 deep-well heat block. Select the MVRI_Flex_SV_300ul protocol on the equipment and load the plates onto the King Fisher Flex as directed, then start the protocol.

steps.title: Close the front lid of the device
steps.instruction: Close the front lid of the device.

steps.title: Label and prepare the processing plates...
steps.instruction: Label and prepare the processing plates according to the table below: Using an automated multidispenser pipette with appropriate combitips tips or the use of multichannel pipettes is recommended for the following steps for maintaining accuracy with solutions containing alcohols and to reduce repetitive strain. Adjust volume and use a new tip for each solution.

steps.title: Add 200 μl of prepared wash...
steps.instruction: Add 200 μl of prepared wash solution 1 to the 96 deep well plates marked FW1 and SW

steps.title: Add 300 μl of prepared wash...
steps.instruction: Add 300 μl of prepared wash solution 2 of the 96 deep well plates marked FW2 and SW

steps.title: Add 60 μl of nuclease free...
steps.instruction: Add 60 μl of nuclease free water to wells of the 96-standard plate marked Elution.

steps.title: Place a 96 tip comb in a 96-standard plate
steps.instruction: Place a 96 tip comb in a 96-standard plate.

steps.title: After completion of the run, a final prompt will appear
steps.instruction: After completion of the run, a final prompt will appear. “Unload plate containing the RNA”.

steps.title: Transfer eluted viral RNA to labelled...
steps.instruction: Transfer eluted viral RNA to labelled containers and keep on ice for immediate use or store frozen until needed.

steps.title: Empty and wipe clean equipment
steps.instruction: Empty and wipe clean equipment. Dispose of processing plates and their contents by following standard laboratory processes.

steps.title: Set the thermal block to 70°C
steps.instruction: Set the thermal block to 70°C.

steps.title: For each sample
steps.instruction: For each sample: Set up and label clean nuclease free 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes for each FTA card to be processed.

steps.title: Open one FTA card pouch and take out the card
steps.instruction: Open one FTA card pouch and take out the card. Place the cutting mat under the FTA card marked area, where the paper will be punched.

steps.title: Using a new 4mm disposable punch,...
steps.instruction: Using a new 4mm disposable punch, punch out seven (7) punches per sample and place in the labelled 1.5 ml microtube. (Do not punch too hard or the punch will dull and not cut). Avoid moving the FTA card around on the cutting mat, so as not to contaminate the cutting surface. Ensure FTA card is punched in a manner to allow for additional round of extraction if necessary with positioning the punched holes accordingly.

steps.title: Dispose of the biopsy punch in...
steps.instruction: Dispose of the biopsy punch in the sharps bin and use new punch for each card.

steps.title: Place the next card in a...
steps.instruction: Place the next card in a different spot on the cutting mat. The front and back of the mat may be used before having to clean the mat. (Do not use the same spot as before to punch a new card to avoid contamination between cards).

steps.title: Aliquot 300 μl of RNA Processing...
steps.instruction: Aliquot 300 μl of RNA Processing Buffer into each tube with the FTA card discs.

steps.title: Close and vortex tubes at high speed for 40 seconds
steps.instruction: Close and vortex tubes at high speed for 40 seconds.

steps.title: Incubate tubes at 70°C for 5...
steps.instruction: Incubate tubes at 70°C for 5 minutes at 800 rpm speed. (If not using thermomixer, take tubes and mix by vortexing briefly at moderate speed every 2 minutes to a total of 3 times.) Ensure the tube lids are properly closed during incubation to prevent popping open during mixing.

steps.title: When incubation is over, check the...
steps.instruction: When incubation is over, check the lids are closed properly, vortex and centrifuge tubes briefly to collect content. (You will be able to recover up to ~240 µl of supernatant for RNA extraction from each sample). Process sample supernatants to extract viral RNA by following one of the extraction workflows in step 3/4/5.
